source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Green Canyon Block 18

event.top_event: Amputation of fingers due to falling wireline tool and pack-off assembly
actions_taken: Medivac flight arranged for further medical treatment
Updated JSA to include Hands-Free Work Objective
Created pre-job planning checklist for 3rd party activities
Re-issued Hands-Free training and personnel hoisting process
event.summary: On August 13, 2020, a Floorhand working in a riding belt on the H&P 100 rig was hoisted to guide wireline tool string out of a lubricator. The wireline pulled free from the rope socket after the dual pack-off snagged on a shackle, causing the 65-pound pack-off assembly to fall and amputate the Floorhand's thumb and two fingers on his left hand. The incident resulted from improper hand placement in violation of H&P's Hands-Free Work Objective, lack of communication of this policy in pre-tour meetings, and failure of crew members to stop work when they observed the unsafe hand placement.
recommendations: Update JSA to outline H&P responsibilities during rigging up/down of 3rd party wireline equipment
Include Hands-Free policy, No Go Zones, and prohibition on lifting additional tools with wireline tool in JSA
Create pre-job planning checklist for 3rd party activities
Re-issue Hands-Free training and personnel hoisting process for all Offshore Division rigs

hazards.name: Hoisted load (wireline tool and pack-off assembly)
Mechanical failure of rope socket connection
Personnel in proximity to hoisted loads

threats.name: Rope socket failure due to pack-off snagging on shackle
Floorhand hand placement in line of fire of hoisted load
Failure to communicate and enforce Hands-Free Work Objective

consequences.name: Permanent disability and loss of hand function

controls.name: H&P Offshore Hands-Free Work Objective policy
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent personnel from placing hands on hoisted loads
controls.lod_basis: Policy designed to prevent contact with hoisted loads

supporting_text: H&P has an Offshore Hands-Free Work Objective which prohibits workers from placing their hands or fingers on any hoisted loads
all crew members were trained on this objective
The Floorhand that was in the riding belt was using his left hand to guide the tool string out of the lubricator
The Driller and other crew members witnessed the Floorhand reach out to guide the tool string out but did not stop the job in accordance with the Offshore Hands-Free Objective

controls.name: Pre-tour Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for wireline operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and communicate safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: JSA intended to communicate hazards and controls before work commences

supporting_text: the Rig Manager led the day crew through a pre-tour Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the day's operation, which included wireline/E-line operations
Hands Free Work Objective, Hand placement, and Line of Fire potential were not addressed in pre-tour or C & D wireline JSEA
the drill crew involved in the rig down did not sign the C&D wireline JSEA or discuss H&P's Hands-Free Work Objective in the pre-tour meeting

controls.name: C&D Wireline Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to communicate contractor hazards and procedures
controls.lod_basis: JSEA intended to document contractor-specific hazards and controls

supporting_text: The C&D Supervisor printed a Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA). The supervisor, his employee, and tool technician reviewed and signed the document
C&D wireline JSEA missing step for pulling tool out of lubricator
Drill crew reviewed but did not sign the C&D wireline JSEA prior to commencing work

controls.name: Supervision and work observation by Driller and crew
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to monitor work and enforce safety policies
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory oversight to detect and stop unsafe acts

supporting_text: The Driller and other crew members witnessed the Floorhand reach out to guide the tool string out but did not stop the job in accordance with the Offshore Hands-Free Objective
Driller and crew witnessed Floorhand reach out for the tool but did not stop the job in accordance to H&P Offshore Hands Free Policy

controls.name: Rope socket connection integrity
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to maintain mechanical integrity of wireline attachment
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical design to prevent unintended load release

supporting_text: the dual pack-off is believed to have gotten snagged on the shackle located on the rig hoist line which suspended the lubricator in the derrick. This allowed the wireline to pull out of the rope socket
The dual pack-off snagged on a shackle, which over powered the knot on the rope socket and caused the wireline to pull free

controls.name: Personnel Hoisting JSA and H&P Pre-Job Checklist
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to communicate personnel hoisting hazards and procedures
controls.lod_basis: JSA and checklist intended to identify and control personnel hoisting hazards

supporting_text: the H&P day crew paused to review a JSA on Personnel Hoisting with Manrider winch, along with a H&P Pre-Job Checklist for Personnel Hoisting. All documents were signed by the day crew
Hands Free Work Objective, Hand placement, and Line of Fire potential were not addressed in pre-tour or C & D wireline JSEA

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA) implementation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe work and prevent injury escalation
controls.lod_basis: SWA activated after incident to prevent further harm

supporting_text: someone shouted an all stop
A Stop Work Authority (SWA) was initiated and the Floorhand was lowered to the rig floor and escorted to the medic's office

controls.name: On-scene medical response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative/medical control to provide emergency treatment and evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Medical response to minimize injury severity and facilitate treatment

supporting_text: The Floorhand was treated by the medic on-scene and sent in on a Medivac flight for further medical treatment
the Floorhand was lowered to the rig floor and escorted to the medic's office. He was later sent in on a medivac flight for further medical treatment

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
